D5 Design L1 Remember Atom

Aristotle's Line of Colour

before the colour wheel there was a colour line — slide along Aristotle's single scale from white through to black

For two thousand years, following Aristotle, colour was thought to be one-dimensional: a single ladder running from white down to black, with all hues strung between as degrees of brightness. There was no circle, no separate "hue" — only lighter and darker.

Slide along the line and read where each colour was placed. The idea that colour needs more than one axis — that hue is independent of brightness — did not arrive until Newton bent the line into a wheel.
